one_time_description: Resources revenue of $6,830,802 in 2023 vs $2,736,154 in 2022 - likely includes significant resource revenue sharing; also qualified opinion due to non-consolidation of Treaty Land Entitlement Trust and non-adoption of PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations
economic_notes: First Nation has 10% investment in Amik-BBF HydroKap LP and Amik-CTR HydroKap LP; 33% investment in 2454357 Ontario Inc. and indirect 33% in Three Nations Drilling LP. COFN TLE trust distributions received. Significant resource revenue from Goldcorp Borden Limited and Three Nations Drilling LP.
notes: Qualified audit opinion for two reasons: (1) Chapleau Ojibwe First Nation Treaty Land Entitlement Trust (created 2017) not consolidated as required under PSAS; (2) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not adopted effective April 1, 2022 as required. Four subsidiaries fully consolidated (two limited partnerships and two corporations related to Hydrokap projects). Resources revenue jumped from $2.7M to $6.8M year over year - likely from Three Nations Drilling LP and Goldcorp Borden. Demand loans of $61,484 classified as current liability. Long-term debt of $161,444 is entire RBC mortgage matures December 2023 - all classified as current. Capital segment shows negative expenses due to contracted services credit of ($149,934).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
